design_philosophy.core_loop: Jump, attack with varying magic levels, collect items to restore health/magic, defeat bosses across nine worlds to rescue Kitty from the Professor
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Magic power management as a health buffer—taking hits depletes magic levels before losing a life, creating resource tension
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as the classic Felix the Cat character, using magic powers to navigate colorful worlds and rescue Kitty
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: A fairly easy, short platformer that prioritizes accessible gameplay over difficulty. The magic level system creates a forgiving damage model where players get multiple hits before losing a life. Enemies follow predictable patterns rather than aggressive AI, emphasizing pattern recognition over reaction speed.

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system; Felix loses a life when falling into bottomless pits, running out of time, or losing all health
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; difficulty is fixed. The magic power system provides a forgiving damage model where multiple hits are absorbed before life loss, but players cannot adjust challenge.

progression.upgrade_system: Magic level system—Felix has nine magic levels that determine attack type. Magic levels decrease when hit and increase when collecting magic items. Magic power also serves as health buffer.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Nine sequential worlds that must be completed in order to defeat the Professor and rescue Kitty
replayability_hooks: Completion is described as 'somewhat short,' suggesting quick replay potential
Different magic levels provide varied attack types, encouraging exploration of different power states
Game Boy version features fewer levels than NES version, offering alternative experience

world_building_techniques: Simple rescue narrative framing: Professor kidnaps Kitty, Felix must save her
Japanese text exists in cutscenes in international ROM, suggesting story moments were included despite being optional
Character-based charm from Felix the Cat license carries thematic weight

boss_design.philosophy: Enemies follow 'regular pace back and forth' suggesting predictable, pattern-based design rather than adaptive difficulty. Eight boss monsters distributed across nine worlds.

danger_signals: Enemies: moles, tree trunks, cannons, birds, fish
Environmental hazards: bottomless pits, time limits
environmental_storytelling: Nine distinct worlds with thematic enemy types (water levels with fish, tree-themed areas with trunk obstacles, etc.)
Scattered items signaling resource availability and level progression

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Felix the Cat NES article)
wikipedia (Felix the Cat video game article)
